title: Vectors — Lists of Numbers with Direction (Beginner)
key_insight: A vector is just a list of numbers. In AI, it represents anything — a word, an image, a concept.
body:
## What is a Vector?

```python
import numpy as np

position   = np.array([3, 4])      # 2D location
colour     = np.array([255, 128, 0])  # RGB
word_embed = np.array([0.3, -0.1, 0.8])  # word meaning
```

## Dot Product — Measuring Similarity

```python
a = np.array([1, 2, 3])
b = np.array([4, 5, 6])

dot = np.dot(a, b)  # (1×4)+(2×5)+(3×6) = 32
# Or:
dot = a @ b
```

Large positive dot product = vectors point in similar directions = similar meaning.

## Word Embeddings

```python
# Words are vectors — similar words have similar vectors
# king - man + woman ≈ queen
# This is how word2vec and all modern embeddings work
```
common_mistakes: Confusing a vector with just any list — vectors represent direction and magnitude
Forgetting dot product requires same-length vectors
Thinking dot product = vector length — it's a scalar measuring relationship between two vectors
mini_exercise: Create two vectors: a=[1,0] (pointing right) and b=[0,1] (pointing up). Compute their dot product — should be 0 (perpendicular = unrelated). Then try a=[1,0] and c=[1,0]. Dot product = 1 (identical direction).
